title: Garlicky Fluffy French Lentils Quiche
description: A quick‑fix, vegetarian French quiche brimming with garlicky lentils and a fluffy, cheese‑y custard, finished with a splash of white wine vinegar.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water and place them in a pot with 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 15‑20 minutes until tender but not mushy. Drain any excess liquid.
While the lentils cook, heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and thyme‑rosemary blend to the skillet, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Fold the cooked lentils into the skillet, stir to combine, and season with a dash of black pepper. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and white wine vinegar until well blended.
Lay the shortcrust pastry on a baking sheet or a quiche tin, trimming any excess. Spoon the lentil mixture into the pastry shell, then pour the egg custard over it.
Sprinkle the grated Gruyère cheese evenly across the top.
Bake for 25‑30 minutes, or until the custard is set and the cheese is golden brown.
Remove from the oven, let cool for a few minutes, then slice and serve warm.
